attributes: celebrated as the enemy of the Agha demon

1.verse_text: The highwaymen lust, greed and anger have captured me and bound me with the ropes of the desire for fame. I pray that the heroic devotees of Lord Krishna, the enemy of the Agha demon, may defeat my captors and cut the ropes that bind me.

attributes: bestower of the nectarean potion of pure love for Him
the Divine Lord to whom the speaker prays for devotional service

2.verse_text: I am scorched by the blazing forest fire of old age, deeply bitten by the poisonous snake of blindness, pierced by the sharp arrows of forced dependence on others, and surrounded by the lions of anger, lust, and greed. O Lord Krishna, please give me the nectarean potion of pure love for You, which will make me strong and heroic enough to resist all the enemies and engage in Your devotional service without deviation.

attributes: possessors of a transcendental nectar ocean of sweetness
sacred land that is the abode of the divine couple

3.verse_text: By tasting a single drop of the memory of the transcendental nectar ocean of the sweetness of the divine couple my heart has become restless. I bow down before the land and people of Vraja. I yearn to see my two masters.

attributes: crowning garlands of all charming couples
objects of the narrator's longing to lovingly gaze upon
their sight provides nectar continuously tasted by the demigod cupid
land eternally illuminated by the disembodied demigod cupid
site of a jubilant festival of Lord Kṛṣṇa's transcendental amorous pastimes
His transcendental amorous pastimes constitute a jubilant festival in Vraja

4.verse_text: The disembodied demigod cupid, who attained a body by continually tasting the nectar of the sight of the divine couple, eternally illuminates this entire land of Vraja, with a jubilant festival of Lord Krsna’s transcendental amorous pastimes. When shall I lovingly gaze on Sri Sri Gandharva-Giridhari (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna), the crowning garlands of all charming couples.

attributes: city where the brother and sons of the Lord reside
place where the Lord enjoys pastimes with a multitude of queens
city dearer even than the spiritual realm of Vaikuntha
Lord who enjoys transcendental pastimes with a multitude of queens
the Lord whose birth occurred in the city of Mathura
the abode of transcendental love dearer than Dvaraka
the sacred place of Lord Krsna's transcendental birth
eternal object of the narrator's worship
land splendidly manifest within the district of Mathura

5.verse_text: The city of Dvaraka, where the brother and sons of the Lord reside, and where the lord enjoys transcendental pastimes with a great multitude of queens, each eclipsing many hundreds of goddesses of fortune, is dearer even than Vaikuntha. Because it is the place of Lord Krsna’s birth, Mathura, the abode of transcendental love, is even more dear than Dvaraka. Within the district of Mathura , the land of Sri Vraja is splendidly manifest. I eternally worship the land of Mathura.

attributes: enjoys the very sweet nectar of transcendental pastimes
herds the cows with His brother and many dear friends
affectionate personality who engages in pastimes in Vraja
brother of Śrī Kṛṣṇa who herds cows with Him
place where nectar of transcendental pastimes is enjoyed
place that is more dear even than the city of Mathura
city less dear than the holy land of Vraja

6.verse_text: Even today affectionate Lord Krsna enjoys the very sweet nectar of transcendental pastimes there, herding the cows with His brother and His many dear friends. A wonderful sweetness becomes manifest in the heart of they who understand the nectar of this place. Of Vraja, which is more dear even than the city of Mathura, I take shelter.

attributes: youthful pair surrounded by their witty gopi friends
enjoys many kinds of transcendental pastimes in Vṛndāvana
leaves the scent of their lotus feet upon the forest
delightful with the scent of the divine couple's lotus feet
contains groves, mountain caves, and blossoming vines for pastimes

7.verse_text: Surrounded by their witty gopi friends, the youthful divine couple enjoys many kinds of transcendental pastimes within the groves and mountain caves and among the blossoming vines of Vrndavana forest. I worship Vrndavana forest, which is delightful with the scent of their lotus feet.

attributes: town in Vṛndāvana where the goddess of fortune always walks
place where the great mystic perfections are eternally present
sacred town where the cows of Vraja are protected
home of the people of Vraja and the king of Vraja

enjoys transcendental pastimes under the loving protection of His parents
king of Vraja residing in the town of Nandisvara

8.verse_text: In Vrndavana is the town of Nandisvara, where the goddess of fortune always walks, where the great mystic perfections are eternally present, where the cows are protected, where the people of Vraja live, and where Lord Krsna happily enjoys transcendental pastimes of under the loving protection of His parents. I worship this town of Nandisvara, the home of the king of Vraja and the most important of the land of Vraja.

attributes: king of Gokula who celebrated the birth of his son
gave charity to brahmanas including palatable foods and nectar beverages

son of the King of Gokula for whom blessings were begged

9.verse_text: To celebrate his son’s birth, the King of Gokula respectively gave in charity to the brahmanas palatable foods, nectar beverages, and transcendental cows decorated with many jewels. Day after day he satisfied the brahmanas with these valuable presentations, and he repeatedly begged for their blessings on his infant boy. I worship the king of Gokula, who is always absorbed in thoughts of his son.

attributes: queen of Vraja whose breasts continually trickle milk out of maternal love
prepared to give her life millions of times to protect her son
deeply attached to her son Kṛṣṇa
cries like a cow frightened for her newborn calf if she cannot see her son
son of Yaśodā-devī who is the object of her intense maternal affection

10.verse_text: May the queen of Vraja, whose breasts continually trickle milk out of maternal love for her son, who is prepared to give her life many millions of times to protect him from even a single drop of perspiration, and who is so attached to Him that if she cannot for a moment see Him she cries like a cow frightened for her new born calf, protect us.

attributes: showers her love upon Lord Kṛṣṇa due to son's intimate friendship
expert cook who provides delicious culinary preparations

queen of Vraja delighted by the friendship and saintly qualities of Rohiṇī-devī

11.verse_text: Because of her own son Balarama’s intimate friendship with Him, Rohini-devi showers her love upon Lord Krsna. She is an expert cook and she delights the king and queen of Vraja with her sincere friendship and saintly qualities.

attributes: splendour of transcendental form eclipses millions of glistening rising moons

never leaves Kṛṣṇa alone in the forest for even a moment out of love
worshiped by prayers and known as the enemy of Dhenukasura

12.verse_text: The splendour of Lord Balarama’s transcendental form eclipses many millions of glistening rising moons, and the slightest scent of His boundless strength is sufficient to destroy many armies of demons. Although He knows the supernatural power of his younger brother, Krsna, still, out of love for Him, He never leaves Him alone in the forest for even a moment. I offer my prayers to Lord Balarama, the enemy of Dhenukasura.

attributes: speaks with a voice that mocks the roaring of hundreds of thousands of thunderclouds
expresses deep pride in having such a wonderful grandson
expert at delighting his grandson by speaking many joking words into His ear
grandson of Parjanya Maharaja who is delighted by his grandfather's joking words

13.verse_text: Speaking with a voice that seems to mock the roaring of hundreds of thousands of thunderclouds, Parjanya Maharaja expresses his pride in having such a wonderful grandson. I offer my respectful obeisances to Parjanya, Krsna’s grandfather, who is expert at delighting his grandson by speaking into His ear many joking words.

attributes: paternal grandmother of Kṛṣṇa
worships Kṛṣṇa with merry joking words

addressed by his grandmother as the moon of grandsons

14.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to Variyasi-devi, Krsna’s grandmother. With merry joking words she worships Krsna, calling Him the moon of grandsons. Happy and proud of her grandson her feet no longer touch the ground when she walks.

15.verse_text: His face handsome with a great white beard, and his complexion dark, pious Upananda is the respected prime minister in the council chambers of the King of Vraja. Considering Him millions of times more dear than his own life, Upananda delights his nephew Krsna. I pray that Upananda may always stay in the government council and protect the land of Vraja.

attributes: very gentle, affectionate, and generous younger brother of Nanda Maharaja

16.verse_text: Nanda Maharaj’s younger brother Sannanda is very gentle, affectionate and generous. His complexion is fair and his beard black. He is very devoted to his elder brother and he is the father of Sunanda-gopi. He pleases Krsna by giving Him palatable dishes made with buffalo yoghurt. He worships Krsna with every breath. May Lord Krsna’s uncle Sannanda, the master of a great herd of buffalo, always protect us.

attributes: dark complexioned, youthful, handsome, and intelligent son of Upananda
best of astrologers who has defeated even Brhaspati with his scholarship

king of Vraja who has Subhadra at his left hand
protected by the good advice of Subhadra

17.verse_text: Upananda’s son Subhadra is dark complexioned, youthful, handsome, and intelligent. He is the best of astrologers. With his vast scholarship he has defeated even Brhaspati. He stays at the left hand of the king of Vraja. With good advice he protects Lord Krsna, who is millions of times more dear to him than his own life. With great pleasure let us glorify, offer our respectful obeisances to Subhadra.

attributes: frightened that delicate Kṛṣṇa may be hurt by demons

18.verse_text: Frightened that delicate Krsna may be hurt by the demons, Ambika performed severe austerities for many days to please the goddess Durga, who granted her a boon that her son Vijaya would become a powerful hero able to kill many demons. May Ambika, Lord Krsna’s nurse, protect us.

attributes: calls out with a voice heard in millions of spiritual universes

son of Kṛṣṇa's nurse

19.verse_text: Saying “Where are the demons? With ease I shall shorten their lives,” Vijaya calls so loudly his words are heard in millions of spiritual universes. Out of maternal affection for Krsna, Ambika instructed her son Vijaya to always stay near child Krsna and protect Him from the demons. I worship Vijaya, the son of Krsna’s nurse.

attributes: learned chief priest of Vraja-pura and personification of all the Vedas

20.verse_text: Bhaguri Muni, the learned chief priest of Vraja-pura, is the personification of all the Vedas. Everyday He chants mantras for the protection of each of Krsna’s limbs. Everyday he smells Krsna’s head. Everyday he offers Krsna many benedictions. I bow down and offer my respectful obeisances to Bhaguri Muni, the best of the sages.

21.verse_text: Sridama is the most expert and clever of the cowherd boys, and he is the abode of intense love for Lord Krsna. His complexion is dark, and he is proud that his qualities, age, clothing and handsome features closely resemble that of Lord Krsna. If for a single moment he cannot see Lord Krsna, he trembles with anxiety. I completely take shelter of Sridama, the constant companion of Lord Krsna.

attributes: filled with deep love for Śrī Kṛṣṇa and anxious about separation

objects of Subala's love that causes his body to tremble

22.verse_text: Filled with deep love for him, and anxious that they may be separated, Subala never, even in dream, lets go of the hand of Sri Krsna, the moon of Gokula. Subala’s heart is sprinkled with the waters of the swiftly moving mountain stream of devotion to Srimati Radhika. I offer my respectful obeisances to Subala whose body trembles with love for Sri Sri Radha-Krsna.

attributes: Lord whose presence inspires play and joking in His friends

sacred cows assembled in one place by Kṛṣṇa's friends

23.verse_text: Assembling the surabhi cows in one place, hand in hand, Krsna’s friends play and joke. For them the ocean of pure love has washed away the mud of awe and reverence in Krsna’s presence. Let us take shelter of these boys, whose hearts, lives, and property are all placed at Lord Krsna’s feet.

attributes: personified mellow of comedy who is pure-hearted and eternally hungry

24.verse_text: The personified mellows of comedy, pure-hearted, eternally hungry, jubilant, and splendid Madhumangala daily induces the laughter of his two friends (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna) with a host of comic speeches and gestures. With great devotion let us at once bow down to offer our respect to Madhumangala, the joking friend of Lord Krsna, the moon of Vrndavana.

attributes: noble and auspicious personality who is worshipped with deep devotion

whose pastimes provide nectar happiness to Paurnamasi

25.verse_text: Accompanied by an expert gopi friend, Paurnamasi daily arranges the secret rendezvous of expert Sri Sri Radha and Krsna. I worship noble and auspicious Paurnamasi, who at every moment tastes the nectar happiness of seeing the pastimes of the divine couple.

26.verse_text: Noble, generous, learned, intelligent, fair complexion, fifty-year-old King Vrsabhanu wears a neatly clipped beard. He is the close friend of Nanda, the king of Vraja. To him, Sri Radha is even dearer than his eldest child Sridama. I constantly worship famous King Vrsabhanu.

mother of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
daughter of Kirtida-devi whose recent activities are being monitored

27.verse_text: Her mind overwhelmed with a flood of love, Kirtida-devi daily sends her two unmarried daughters to diligently gather all news of Srimati Radharani’s recent activities. May Kirtida-devi, the mother of Srimati Radharani protect us all.

enjoys transcendental happiness increased by the obstacles of Mukhara-devi

28.verse_text: Angrily placing many obstacles to stop Their amorous pastimes, Mukhara-devi only increases the transcendental happiness of her two young grandchildren, Sri Sri Radha and Krsna. I respectfully bow down before elderly Mukhara-devi, and I place her lotus feet upon my head.

29.verse_text: Flooded with the nectar of intense love for her two friends (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna), who more dear to her than her own life, affectionately arrogant in their presence, and daily arranging their rendezvous, Lalita-devi expertly teaches her friend Radha the art of jealous anger. I pray Lalita-devi will one day accept me as one of her associates.

attributes: place where youthful divine couple enjoys affectionate and playful joking pastimes
possesses sweet transcendental singing that eclipses the voices of cuckoos
youthful couple who enjoys affectionate and playful joking pastimes

30.verse_text: Visakha-devi is the place where youthful divine couple enjoys affectionate and playful joking pastimes. Her sweet transcendental singing eclipses the voices of the cuckoos. I pray that Vishaka-devi may become pleased with me and accept me as her student.

attributes: filled with a great flood of love
decorating the ever-blossoming groves of Vrndavana with many fragrant flowers
creates a festive atmosphere for the transcendental pastimes of the divine couple
abode of ever-blossoming groves decorated with many fragrant flowers
enjoy transcendental pastimes with their dear friends in Vrndavana

31.verse_text: Filled with a great flood of love and decorating the ever-blossoming groves of Vrndavana with many fragrant flowers, Vrnda-devi creates a festive atmosphere for Sri Sri Radha-Krsna to enjoy transcendental pastimes with Their dear friends. Let me surrender to Vrnda-devi.

narrates the latest news of Lord Krsna to Srimati Radharani
glows with friendship and auspicious playfulness
becomes filled with happiness while walking on the path
brought by Kundalata-devi to cook by the order of the queen of Vraja

queen of Vraja who ordered Kundalata-devi to bring Srimati Radharani to cook
subject of the latest news narrated by Kundalata-devi

32.verse_text: Bringing Srimati Radharani to cook by the order of the queen of Vraja, and, as they both walked together on the path pleasing her by affectionately and continually narrating the latest news of Lord Krsna charming Kundalata-devi, glowing with friendship and auspicious playfulness became filled with happiness. I worship Kundalata-devi.

attributes: brought by the queen of Vraja to cook many palatable dishes
affectionate personality who uses many clever tricks
happily arranged for Srimati Radharani to meet her lover Krsna
considers her friend Radha to be more dear than life
queen of Vraja who brings Dhanistha-devi to cook palatable dishes

lover of Krsna who meets Him in the forest groves
lover of Srimati Radharani meeting in the forest groves
location of the forest groves where the divine couple meets

33.verse_text: Brought by the queen of Vraja to cook many palatable dishes of her own accord affectionate Dhanistha-devi, using many clever tricks, happily arranged for Srimati Radharani to meet her lover, Krsna, in the forest groves of Nandisvara Hill. I worship Dhanistha-devi, for whom her friend Radha is more dear than life.

attributes: heart enchanted by repeatedly hearing the glories of the divine couple
longing to meet the divine couple

subjects of nectarean and splendid pastimes in Vraja
recipients of the increased happiness provided by Nandimukhi-devi
land to which Nandimukhi-devi traveled to serve the divine couple

34.verse_text: Her heart enchanted by repeatedly hearing the glories of the divine couple and longing to meet Them, Nandimukhi-devi left Avantipura and went to the land of Vraja, where she eternally increases the happiness of Sri Sri Radha and Krsna’s splendid and nectarean pastimes. I eternally offer my respectful obeisances to Nandimukhi-devi.

35.verse_text: Her waters filled with musk and saffron fallen from Sri Sri Radha-Krsna as they enjoy many nectar water-pastimes, the Jamuna river happily smiles at Them with her foam and splashes Them with the beautiful hands of her waves. I pray the Jamuna river may bring transcendental happiness to us.

enjoys transcendental pastimes with Lord Kṛṣṇa

36.verse_text: Moved by the appeal of supremely blissful Lord Krsna, the gopis spontaneously and angrily rebuke Srimati Radharani, who is more dear to them than their own life’s breath and encourage her to again enjoy the pastimes with Krsna. Bowing my head I take shelter of these dear friends of Srimati Radharani, who daily arranged for her to enjoy transcendental pastimes with Lord Krsna.

receives service from his servants who act like friends
pleased by servants bringing the flute, vina, water, and betel nuts

37.verse_text: With great love, enthusiasm, and respect, Krsna’s servants, who are much like his friends, serve Him in many ways and please Him by bringing the flute, vina, water, betel nuts, and other articles. I worship Krsna’s servants who have Patri as their leader.

38.verse_text: By offering her betel nuts, by massaging her feet, by bringing her water, by arranging for her meetings with Lord Krsna, and by performing many other services, many gopi maidservants affectionately please Sri Radha, the great queen of Vrndavana forest. When the Divine Couple enjoy Their pastimes, these maidservants are not at all shy, even in the presence of the great gopis for whom Sri Radha is more dear than life. I take shelter of these gopi maidservants, who have Srimati Rupa-manjari as their leader.

attributes: enemy of the Mura demon
husband of the goddess of fortune

filled with pure love for Lord Kṛṣṇa

39.verse_text: Considering the expanded ocean of their own happiness to be as insignificant as a blade of grass and filled with pure love for Lord Krsna, the enemy of the Mura demon, the Lord’s personal associates strive to make him always happy. I worship these fortunate associates of Lord Krsna, the husband of the goddess of fortune.

attributes: source of happiness for her personal associates
beloved more than their own lives by her personal associates
pleased when Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī becomes happy

love Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī more than their own lives

40.verse_text: Let me worship the personal associates of Srimati Radharani. When Radharani becomes happy, they are pleased. If for a moment they cannot see Radharani, they become so distraught that they appear almost dead. They must have performed some pious activities in their previous births in order to obtain this auspicious condition where they love Srimati Radharani more than their own lives.

attributes: sometimes becomes proud of her own good fortune

briefly enjoys pastimes with other gopis to increase Rādhārāṇī's jealous love
blossom with many amorous desires
must have performed many great pious activities to play this role

41.verse_text: In order to increase the splendid and nectarean jealous love of Srimati Radharani, who sometimes becomes proud of her own good fortune, Lord Krsna briefly enjoys pastimes with other gopis, who blossom with many amorous desires. Let us offer our respectful obeisances to these gopis, who must have performed many great pious activities in order to play this role in the Lord’s pastimes.

attributes: affectionately protects the cowherd boys of Vraja
possessor of handsome lotus toenails from which dust particles fall
affectionately protected by the personality of Śrī Kṛṣṇa
hearts are rapt in meditation on the dust from Kṛṣṇa's toenails
experience happiness greater than that of many millions of universes

42.verse_text: Affectionately protected by Krsna, and their hearts rapt in meditation on the particles of dust falling from the tip of his handsome lotus toenails, the gopas experience a happiness greater than the happiness of many millions of universes. I worship these exalted gopas.

attributes: worshipped by elderly gopis who consider Him dearer than their own lives
has lotus feet from which a drop of perspiration is worshipped
has a head decorated with handsome locks of hair
consider Kṛṣṇa dearer than their own lives out of parental love
worship a drop of perspiration from the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa
kiss the head of Kṛṣṇa which is decorated with handsome locks of hair

43.verse_text: Considering Him dearer than their own lives out of parental love, the elderly gopis worship even a drop of perspiration of Krsna’s lotus feet, and for a long time they kiss his head, decorated with handsome locks of hair. I worship the dust of these gopis’ feet.

attributes: surabhi cows of Śrī Kṛṣṇa with hooves decorated with sapphires
possessors of horns studded with gold
possessors of white cheeks that have crippled the pride of snow-capped mountain peaks
owner of the surabhi cows

44.verse_text: The hooves of Sri Krsna’s surabhi cows are decorate with sapphires, their horns are studded with gold, and their white cheeks have crippled the pride of the snow-capped mountain peaks. I pray these surabhi cows may protect us.

attributes: prince of Vraja who daily enjoys a great festival of protecting and milking cows
has a body splendidly covered with dust raised by the hooves of cows
eagerly enjoys pastimes with friends in the great forests, grand hills, and river banks of Vraja

cows whose hooves raise dust that covers the body of the prince of Vraja
objects of the daily festival of protection and milking by the prince of Vraja
sacred land featuring great forests, grand hills, and river banks where pastimes occur

45.verse_text: In the company of Balarama and His other friends, and His own body splendidly covered with the dust raised by their hooves, the prince of Vraja daily enjoys a great festival of protecting and milking the cows. With great happiness He eagerly enjoys pastimes with them in the great forests and on the grand hills and river banks of Vraja. Let me worship these surabhi cows.

attributes: pet bull of Lord Kṛṣṇa
has handsome horns covered with gold and studded with jewels
has hooves splendidly decorated with sapphires
has a splendid neck decorated with a swinging garland of reddish flowers
owner of the pet bull Padmagandha

46.verse_text: All glories to Lord Krsna’s pet bull Padmagandha, whose handsome horns are covered with gold and studded with jewels, whose hooves are splendidly decorated with sapphires, and whose splendid neck is decorated with a swinging garland of reddish flowers.

attributes: earnestly feeds calves by placing tender fresh grass in their mouths
very carefully massages the limbs of the calves
owner of the calves that jump and frolic in Vṛndāvana

sacred forest where Kṛṣṇa's calves jump and frolic

47.verse_text: Sometimes Lord Krsna feeds the calves earnestly placing small bunches of tender fresh grass in their mouths, and sometimes He very carefully massages their limbs. I yearn to one day see these calves of Lord Krsna jumping and frolicking in Vrndavana.

attributes: fortunate flute that deeply drinks the nectar of Lord Kṛṣṇa's lips day and night
weakens the pride of Śrīmatī Rādhikā with its transcendental music
possessor of lips whose nectar is drunk by His flute day and night
whose pride is weakened by the transcendental music of Kṛṣṇa's flute

48.verse_text: The fortunate flute deeply drinks the nectar of Lord Krsna’s lips day and night. I offer my respectful obeisances to this flute, which, with it’s transcendental music, weakens the pride of Srimati Radhika.

attributes: possesses proud jealous anger residing within her heart
remains unmoved by the flattering cajoling words of gopi messengers

able to very easily chase away the proud jealous anger of Rādhārāṇī

49.verse_text: Neither the flattering cajoling words spoken by Krsna’s gopi messengers nor the many hints, allegorises, and joking instructions spoken by Krsna’s cowherd friends nor Krsna’s personally falling at her feet and begging Hr to forgive Him nor even the harsh criticisms spoken by her intimate friends who are able to rout the proud jealous anger residing in Srimati Radha’s heart. Only Lord Krsna’s flute, disdaining all previous useless attempts, was able to very easily chase away that jealous anger. Let us glorify Lord Krsna’s exalted and powerful friend, His flute.

attributes: Lord whose flute music pleases the best of the peacocks
delights in enjoying transcendental pastimes in the forest
best of the peacocks who performs a great festival of dancing
dances in the forest by the shore of a lake
subject of the narrator's ardent yearning to see

50.verse_text: Pleased by the music of Lord Krsna’s flute, the best of the peacocks perform a great festival of dancing in the forest by the shore of a lake. Gracefully moving he delights enjoying transcendental pastimes in the forest. Meditating on this, we ardently yearn to see that dancing king of the peacocks.

attributes: full of love for the residents of Vraja
eager to protect the residents of Vraja
respectfully lifted Govardhana Hill for seven days with His graceful hand
lifted by Śrī Kṛṣṇa for seven days using His graceful hand
caves are splendidly decorated with kunkuma particles from Radha-Krsna's pastimes
studded with many beautiful jewels
enjoyed transcendental pastimes with Kṛṣṇa in the caves of Govardhana Hill

51.verse_text: Full of love for the residents of Vraja, and eager to protect Him, Lord Krsna respectfully lifted Govardhana Hill for seven days with His graceful hand. The caves of Govardhana Hill are splendidly decorated with kunkuma particles left when Sri Sri Radha-Krsna enjoyed transcendental pastimes. May Govardhana Hill, which is studded with many beautiful jewels, protect you all.

attributes: area filled with champaka, asoka, mango, kesanagara, and bakula trees
area decorated with lavanga creepers and madhavi creepers
very beautiful and pleasing to the heart
favourite pastime place of the divine couple
area filled with champaka, asoka, mango, kesanagara, and bakula trees
area decorated with lavanga creepers and madhavi creepers
very beautiful and pleasing to the heart
favourite pastime place of the divine couple
possess favourite pastime places at Rādhā-kunda and Śyāma-kunda

52.verse_text: Filled with many champaka trees, beautiful young asoka trees, mango trees, kesanagara trees, bakula trees, lavanga creepers, and madhavi creepers, the area around Radha-kunda and Syama-kunda is very beautiful and pleasing to the heart, and it is the favourite pastime place of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna. Let me take shelter of these two lakes : Radha-kunda and Syama-kunda.

attributes: delightful forest described as beautiful and sacred
not equal to even a single particle of Radha-kunda and Syama-kunda
beautiful hill which is not equal to even a single particle of Radha-kunda and Syama-kunda
sacred lake more dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa than His own life's breath
surpasses the splendor of the delightful forest of Vṛndāvana and Govardhana Hill
sacred lake more dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa than His own life's breath
surpasses the splendor of the delightful forest of Vṛndāvana and Govardhana Hill
holds Radha-kunda and Syama-kunda more dear than His own life's breath

53.verse_text: The delightful forest of Vrndavana, beautiful Govardhana Hill and the nectarean area of the rasa dance, what to speak of other places are not equal to even a single particle of Radha-kunda and Syama-kunda. Let me take shelter of these two lakes, which are more dear to Lord Krsna than His own life’s breath.

attributes: sacred place with a shore containing a grand pavilion made of gold, pearls and precious gems
site of a splendid grove worshiped by the devotees
reside in a grand pavilion by the shore of Rādhā-kunda
consider everything else unimportant while enjoying transcendental pastimes
serve as the two prime ministers of the deity of transcendental love
happily anoint the kingdom of love with the sandal paste of Their pastimes

54.verse_text: By the shore of Radha-kunda is a grand pavilion made of gold, pearls and precious gems. In that pavilion, Sri Sri Radha and Krsna, considering everything else unimportant, enjoy transcendental pastimes as the two prime ministers of the deity of transcendental love, and They happily anoint that kingdom of love with the sandal paste of Their pastimes. I eternally worship the splendid grove by the shore of Radha-kunda.

attributes: splendidly reflected on the shore of the lake of each other’s heart
necklaces lovingly worn by each other
blessed with all good fortune by the creator Lord Brahmā
the resting place of all good opulences in the three worlds
composed of one fair and one dark personality

55.verse_text: The divine couple is splendidly reflected on the shore of the lake of each other’s heart. They are necklaces lovingly worn by each other. The creator Brahma has blessed Them with all good fortune. They are the resting place of all good opulences in the three worlds. When will I be able to directly worship the fair and dark divine couple.

attributes: youthful divine couple who affectionately fulfil each other's transcendental desires
two splendid monarchs of ujjvala-rasa
engage in restless sidelong glances and movements of Their shoulders
possess the nectar of gentle smiles and nectar-like lips
exchange kisses and firm embraces with one another

56.verse_text: With restless sidelong glances, with movements of Their shoulders, with the nectar of gentle smiles, with kissing each other’s nectar lips, and with firm embraces, the youthful divine couple affectionately fulfils each other’s transcendental desires. Let us offer our respectful obeisances to Them, the two splendid monarchs of ujjvala-rasa.

attributes: have eyes of great length with crookedness in the corners

possess charming crookedness in Their gentle words
possess broadness in Their hips
have extraordinary sweetness manifest in all Their limbs
are the eternal splendour eternally worshipped by the narrator

57.verse_text: In Their eyes is great length, in the corners of Their eyes crookedness, in Their chest and breasts largeness, in Their gentle word charming crookedness, in Their hips broadness, and in all Their limbs extraordinary sweetness. I eternally worship the eternal splendour of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna.

attributes: great lake filled with nectar and surrounded by staircases

58.verse_text: When the demon Arista was slain, a great lake named Arista-kunda, filled with nectar and surrounded with staircases, sprang from Lord Krsna’s lotus foot. Because it is the favourite of Sri Radha, this name embraces the name Radha-kunda. I eternally worship Radha-kunda.

attributes: lake filled with many kadamba trees

59.verse_text: On the pretext of going to fetch clear water the lotus-eyed gopis again and again meet Lord Krsna with great happiness and love at Pavana-sarovara lake, which is filled with many kadamba trees and the pleasant humming sounds of many bees. May Pavana-sarovara lake protect us.

attributes: grandfather of Śrī Kṛṣṇa

60.verse_text: Going to the shore of a certain lake, Parjanya Maharaja, who was to become Krsna’s grandfather, fasted, performed austerities, and worshipped Lord Narayana in order to attain a worthy grandson. Partly for this reason Sri Krsna, who is the reservoir of all transcendental virtues and the lifter of Govardhana Hill, appeared as the son of Nanda, the king of Vraja, and the grandson of Parjanya Maharaja. The place where Parjanya Maharaja fasted is famous in this world as Ksunnahara-sarovara (the lake of fasting). I pray that I may take shelter of this sacred place.

61.verse_text: Ordered by Brahma, who spoke from the sky, Paurnamasi-devi crowned Srimati Radharani queen of the beautiful forest of Vrndavana, by jubilantly sprinkling the waters of the Manasa-ganga and other sacred rivers upon Her head in the company of Savitri-devi and other delighted demigoddesses. I pray that sacred place, known as Unmatta-radhasthali (the place where Srimati Radharani became overwhelmed with happiness) may sprinkle some transcendental happiness on me.

site where Lord Kṛṣṇa enjoyed many pastimes with His friends

62.verse_text: Joking and playing, Lord Krsna enjoyed many pastimes with His friends in a small stone pavilion built by expert architects on Nandisvara Hill. Let me take shelter of that pavilion, which is anointed with the transcendental fragrance of Lord Krsna’s feet.

63.verse_text: As he was dancing with the intelligent, graceful, and splendidly beautiful gopis, Sri Krsna suddenly left their company and went with Srimati Radharani to a secluded place and lovingly decorated Her with many flower ornaments. The sweetness of this place astonishes the residents of all the three worlds. I pray that this sacred place, where Lord Krsna performed the rasa dance, may give me shelter.

attributes: flows so swiftly on the side of Govardhana Hill

64.verse_text: The Manasa-ganga River flows so swiftly on the side of Govardhana Hill that it’s waves push the splendid rocks lining it’s shores. I pray the Manasa-ganga River, where Sri Sri Gandharvika-Muravimardana (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna) enjoy jubilant boating pastimes, may give me shelter.

65.verse_text: All of Them decorated with the colourful mineral pigments found there, Lord Krsna and His affectionate friends enjoy pastimes in the hills of Vraja with a great festival of joking words. In the caves of those same hills, Lord Krsna also enjoys many pastimes with Srimati Radharani. Let me worship these, the best of all hills.

attributes: transcendental son of the king of the gopas

king of the gopas and father of Śrī Kṛṣṇa
abode containing broad lakes and streams used for water sports

66.verse_text: While tending the cows in the summer season, Sri Krsna, the transcendental son of the king of the gopas , jubilantly enjoys water sports. Both sides eager for victory, He affectionately sprinkles His charming friends in the broad lakes and streams of Vraja. I eternally worship these transcendental lakes and streams.

attributes: brilliantly playing duets on the vina and flute

67.verse_text: Hearing the sound of Srimati Radharani and Krsna brilliantly playing duets on the vina and flute, the leaders of the deer community become stunned with transcendental happiness and stand still the grass they were formerly chewing now half-falling from their mouths. I pray that those deer who are full of love for the divine couple, may grant transcendental happiness to me.

attributes: their hearts are pleased by the rising waves of madhura-rasa
youthful couple enjoying transcendental pastimes in the beautiful groves of Vraja

forest groves filled with many humming bees

68.verse_text: Their hearts pleased by the rising waves of madhura-rasa, the youthful divine couple enjoys transcendental pastimes in the beautiful groves of Vraja, aromatic with many pleasant flowers and filled with many humming bees. Let us sweep these forest groves with our long hair.

attributes: youthful divine couple enjoying transcendental pastimes in the forest
seated on a throne glistening with fallen pollen
served by splendid bees carrying drops of honey
provide a very cool and pleasant shade
surround the throne where the divine couple enjoys pastimes

69.verse_text: In the very cool and pleasant shade of the trees, on a throne glistening with fallen pollen and served by splendid bees carrying drops of honey, the youthful divine couple enjoy transcendental pastimes. I pray these trees may always protect me.

70.verse_text: As They pick flowers, the gently smiling lovers, Sri Sri Gandharva-Muravairi (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna), playfully quarrel. Seeing these eternally new and fresh transcendental pastimes, Their gopi friends begin to smile, and the creepers of Vraja, on the pretext of pushing forth new blossoms, also smile and laugh. May I serve these transcendental creepers with great love and devotion.

attributes: youthful couple residing in the land of Vraja
delighted by the sweet singing of birds
plunged into the nectar mellows of pure devotion

dear to the divine couple
land where the divine couple and singing birds reside

71.verse_text: Plunged into the nectar mellows of pure devotion, with very sweet singing the birds delight the nearby youthful couple in the land of Vraja. I pray that these birds, so dear to the divine couple, may delight me with their glance.

attributes: delight the divine couple by softly buzzing and gracefully wandering
wander among the mango, kadamba, bakula, and other trees
wander through the madhavis and other flowering vines
delighted by the soft buzzing and wandering of the bumblebees

72.verse_text: By softly buzzing and gracefully wandering among the mango, kadamba, bakula, and other trees, as well as through the madhavis and other flowering vines, the bumblebees of Vraja delight the divine couple. Let us offer our respectful obeisances to these bees.

attributes: forest king blossoming with happiness
completely captivated by the sidelong glances of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī

supremely opulent tree capable of granting transcendental happiness

73.verse_text: Blossoming with happiness, the forest king Krsna became completely captivated by the sidelong glances of the forest-queen Srimati Radharani, who was also blossoming with happiness as He decorated Her with many blossoming flowers from the king of kadamba trees. I pray that supremely opulent king of kadamba trees may appear before me and grant transcendental happiness to my eyes.

attributes: king of the demigods who was humbled by great fear

74.verse_text: Humbled by great fear, Indra, the king of the demigods, grasped Lord Krsna’s lotus feet and, accompanied by a surabhi cow, performed the coronation festival of bathing them with the water of the celestial Ganges. In this way Lord Krsna’s kingship over the cows became splendidly manifest. I pray that Govinda-kunda, the lake created by that ceremonial bath, may eternally appear before my eyes.

75.verse_text: When Vraja’s king offered a great quantity of foodstuff to Govardhana Hill, Krsna, the enemy of Aghasura, assumed a gigantic form, proclaimed that He was actually Govardhana Hill, invited everyone to ask boons from Him, and, fooling even Srimati Radharani, ate all the offered food. Let me take shelter of the anna-kuta festival, when Lord Krsna enjoyed these pastimes.

attributes: charming and handsome personality enjoying transcendental pastimes

76.verse_text: Charming and handsome Lord Krsna enjoys transcendental pastimes on the summit of Govardhana Hill, the king of mountains. There He splendidly manifest, eternally enjoying the pleasures of a transcendental king. With great love and devotion I worship Govardhana Hill, where Lord Krsna enjoys transcendental pastimes.

attributes: youthful king of amorous heroes eager to enjoy dana-keli pastime

77.verse_text: One day Lord Krsna, the youthful king of amorous heroes, became eager at heart to enjoy the dana-keli pastime. Blocking the path, He forbade Srimati Radharani to pass, proclaimed Himself a toll collector appointed by the king of love, and demanded that she pay a toll of the milk products She was carrying. I sing the glories of Sri Krsna-vedi, the place where the Lord enjoyed these pastimes.

attributes: lake where Lord Kṛṣṇa enjoyed the dāna-kelī pastime

78.verse_text: Because Sri Krsna enjoyed the dana-keli pastime in a secluded place by the shore of this lake, it has become known as Dana-sarovara. The truth of this hill remains hidden to persons averse to the mellows of pure devotional service. Only the devotees have the power to understand it. I pray that I may reside at Dana-sarovara, the place where Lord Krsna enjoyed the dana-keli pastime.

transcendental lake manifested as a beautiful jewel around Govardhana Hill
transcendental lake manifested as a beautiful jewel around Govardhana Hill
transcendental lake manifested as a beautiful jewel around Govardhana Hill
transcendental lake manifested as a beautiful jewel around Govardhana Hill
transcendental lake manifested as a beautiful jewel around Govardhana Hill
transcendental lake manifested as a beautiful jewel around Govardhana Hill

transcendental lake manifested as a beautiful jewel around Govardhana Hill

79.verse_text: Sri-sarovara, Brahma-sarovara, Kadambakhanda-sarovara, Sumanah-sarovara, Rudra-sarovara, Apsarah-sarovara, Gaurika-sarovara, Jyotsnamoksana-sarovara, Malyahara-sarovara, Vibudhari-sarovara, Indradhvaja-sarovara, and many other beautiful lakes are splendidly manifested as so many beautiful jewels in a circle around divine Govardhana Hill. Let me glorify these transcendental lakes.

attributes: faces blossom with happiness by tasting nectar of swing pastimes
enjoy swing pastimes during the spring season
moved on a swing by the gopis
famous place where the divine couple enjoys swing pastimes

80.verse_text: During the spring season the gopis move Sri Sri Gandharva-Giridhari (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna) on a swing. The faces of the divine couple blossom with happiness by tasting the nectar of these swing pastimes. I worship Govinda-sthala, the famous place where the divine couple enjoys these pastimes.

attributes: punished the Kaliya serpent for offending His friends
danced on and kicked the Kaliya serpent with the soles of His feet
performed pastimes at the Kaliya-hrada lake
offended the dear friends of Śrī Kṛṣṇa
punished by Śrī Kṛṣṇa through dancing and kicking
lake where Śrī Kṛṣṇa performed pastimes of punishing the serpent

81.verse_text: When the Kaliya serpent offended His friends for whom he is more dear than life, Lord Krsna punished him, dancing on him and kicking him with the soles of His feet. I worship Kaliya-hrada, the lake where Lord Krsna performed these pastimes.

attributes: supremely opulent, noble, and magnanimous Lord of Vraja
felt very uncomfortable in the biting winter cold
warmed with great devotion by twelve suns at Dvadasasurya-tirtha
sacred place where twelve suns appeared to warm Kṛṣṇa
bears the name of the place of the twelve suns

82.verse_text: Once, accompanied by the gopas, gopis, and lowing surabhi cows, the supremely opulent, noble, and magnanimous Lord Krsna felt very uncomfortable in the winter cold. Twelve suns then appeared at that place, and with great devotion warmed the Lord with their intense shining. I eternally take shelter of this sacred place, which bears the name Dvadasasurya-tirtha (the place of the twelve suns).

attributes: charming, splendid, and clear lake created from the perspiration of Lord Kṛṣṇa
bears the sweet fragrance of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s delicate, supremely handsome, and supremely opulent transcendental limbs
worshiped by the narrator with bows
possessed of a transcendental body that perspired under the heat of the twelve suns
possessor of delicate, supremely handsome, and supremely opulent transcendental limbs

83.verse_text: When the twelve sums shone from every direction, perspiration fell from Lord Krsna’s transcendental body, and from this perspiration came a charming, splendid, clear lake bearing the sweet fragrance of the Lord’s delicate, supremely handsome, and supremely opulent transcendental limbs. Bowing down, I worship this lake, known as Praskandahana-sarovara.

attributes: possesses clear waters used by the girls of Vraja
site of the incomparable worship of goddess Katyayani
smiling and clever stealer of the Vraja gopis garments
climbed a kadamba tree to observe the Vraja gopis
embarrassed the girls by speaking many splendid joking words
returned the garments to the girls of Vraja
enjoyed intimate pastimes at the shore of Ciraghatta
shoreline location where the Lord enjoyed stealing garments
sacred place providing shelter to the aspiring devotee
left their garments on the shore of the Yamuna
engaged in the incomparable worship of goddess Katyayani
embarrassed by the joking words of clever Kṛṣṇa

84.verse_text: Entering the clear waters of the Yamuna to prepare for the incomparable worship of goddess Katyayani, the girls of Vraja left their garments on the shore. Smiling and clever Krsna stole those garments, climbed a kadamba tree, embarrassed the girls by speaking many splendid joking words, and eventually returned the garments. I take shelter of Ciraghatta, where the Lord enjoyed these pastimes.

attributes: demon who made the three worlds tremble with mad neighing
demon who set the entire universe on flames with wild rolling eyes
killed the demon Keśī as easily as splitting a blade of grass
washed the demon's blood from His hands at Keśī-tīrtha
sacred place where Kṛṣṇa washed blood from His hands
named after the demon Keśī who was killed by Kṛṣṇa

85.verse_text: With his mad neighing, Kesi made the three worlds tremble, and with the wild rolling of his eyes he set the entire universe on flames. Krsna killed this demon as easily as one splits a blade of grass and then washed the blood from His hands at a place now known as Kesi-tirtha. I worship that sacred place.

attributes: beautiful, wealthy, and learned women who reside in Vraja
voluntarily, happily, and devotedly fed Kṛṣṇa and His friends
prepared many varieties of four kinds of foodstuff
created dishes that rebuke the very taste of nectar
enjoyed the pastime of being fed by the wives of the Yājñika-brāhmaṇas
fed many varieties of four kinds of foodstuff with His friends
fed many varieties of four kinds of foodstuff with Kṛṣṇa and friends

entities.display_name: Pastime site of the Yājñika-brāhmaṇa wives
attributes: sacred place where Lord Kṛṣṇa enjoyed the pastime of being fed

86.verse_text: The beautiful, wealthy and learned wives of the yajnika-brahmanas voluntarily, happily and devoted fed Krsna, Balarama, and all their affectionate friends many varieties of the four kinds of foodstuff, each dish rebuking the taste of nectar. We bow down to offer our respects to the place where Lord Krsna enjoyed this pastime.

attributes: splendid and affectionate maidens desiring to attain the treasure of Kṛṣṇa's embraces
devotedly and happily worshipped Lord Śiva on the bank of the Yamunā
possessor of the treasure of embraces desired by the gopīs
worshipped by the gopīs on the bank of the Yamunā
location where the gopīs worshipped Lord Śiva
sacred place where the gopīs performed their worship

87.verse_text: Desiring to quickly attain the treasure of the embraces of Lord Krsna, the splendid and affectionate gopis devotedly and happily worshipped Lord Siva on the bank of the Jamuna. I daily worship Gopisvara-tirtha, where the gopis performed their worship.

attributes: earnestly worshipped Suryadeva in the secluded place of Santanupada
worshipped Suryadeva to attain Kṛṣṇa as Her chosen husband
observed Her father worshipping Suryadeva out of fear of Kamsa
worshipped by Vṛṣabhānu out of fear of Kamsa
earnestly worshipped by Rādhārāṇī to obtain Kṛṣṇa as a husband
deity who hears prayers for protection
father of Rādhārāṇī who worships Sūryadeva out of fear of Kamsa
secluded place where Rādhārāṇī earnestly worshipped Sūryadeva
youth whose complexion is as dark as a host of monsoon clouds
sought as a husband by Rādhārāṇī through worship of Sūryadeva

88.verse_text: Seeing Her father worship Suryadeva out of far of the nearby Kamsa, Srimati Radharani, in the secluded place known as Santanupada, also earnestly worshipped Suryadeva to attain a certain youth, whose complexion is as dark as a host of monsoon clouds, as Her chosen husband. I pray that Suryadeva may protect us.

attributes: king of Vraja who gave charity during the birth festival
respectfully gave brahmanas a mountain of glittering jewel ornaments
gave brahmanas a mountain of grains even greater than the jewels
gave brahmanas 200,000 young cows decorated with gold and pearls
Lord whose birth festival was celebrated with great charity
place where the king of Vraja gave his great charity

89.verse_text: During the great festival of Lord Krsna’s birth, the king of Vraja respectfully gave to the brahmanas a mountain of glittering jewel ornaments, an even greater mountain of grains, and 200,000 young cows, all beautifully decorated with gold and necklaces of large pearls. I offer my respectful obeisances to Mahavana, the place where the king gave his charity.

attributes: precious jewel who took birth in the womb of Kīrtidā-devī
born in the city of Mukhyeravali
mother of Srimati Radharani whose womb is glorified by jubilant demigods, sages and human beings

city where Srimati Radharani took birth
capitol city of King Vrsabhanu
populated with many gopas, gopis, and surabhi cows

90.verse_text: In the city of Mukhyeravali the precious jewel known as Srimati Radharani took birth in the mine of Kirtida-devi’s womb, which is glorified by jubilant demigods, sages and human beings. I pray that when I enter King Vrshabhanu’s capitol city of Mukhyeravali-pura, populated with many gopas, gopis, and surabhi cows, I may become drowned in great flood of transcendental bliss and love.

attributes: yearns for her own happiness to place Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet on her breasts
reluctant to place Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet on her breasts out of fear
possesses a delicate body but fears her breasts are too hard
possesses handsome and delicate lotus feet
places his lotus feet on the hood of Ananta Śeṣa
lord of the land of Vraja
receives the lotus feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa on his hood
the land of Śrī Kṛṣṇa where one seeks to reside

91.verse_text: Although for Her own happiness She yearns to place Lord Krsna’s handsome and delicate lotus feet on her breasts, Srimati Radharani is reluctant to do so, fearing that, even though Her body is very delicate, her breasts are very hard. I pray that Lord Krsna, who also places his lotus feet on the hood of Ananta Sesa, will permit me to reside in His land of Vraja.

attributes: forest that is very dear to Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa
contains the beautiful Kama-sarovara lake
very dear to the forest of Kāmyavana
lake where the gopis enjoy many transcendental pastimes

92.verse_text: Let me worship the forest of Kamyavana. It is very dear to Sri Sri Radha-Krsna, and it contains the Kama-sarovara lake, where the gopis enjoy many transcendental pastimes.

attributes: forest where a wrestling competition occurred among close friends
sacred place worthy of worship by the narrator
beautiful queen who is proud
started a wrestling competition among Her closest friends
eagerly wrestled with Lord Kṛṣṇa
brought great happiness to the god of love through wrestling
engaged in an eager wrestling match with Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī

93.verse_text: In the forest of Bandiravana, my beautiful queen, proud Sri Radha, started a wrestling competition among Her closest friends. When She eagerly wrestled with Lord Krsna, She brought great happiness to the god of love. Let me worship that forest of Bandiravana.

attributes: angry lord who began to drag the Yamuna river
wielder of a plough used to drag the river
possessor of divine power to command the Yamuna river
river that would not come according to Balarama's order
river dragged toward the ocean by Balarama's plough
wonderful site where marks of the dragging river remain
place where men may see marks of Balarama's pastime

94.verse_text: When the Yamuna river would not come according to His order, angry Lord Balarama began to drag it toward the ocean with the edge of His plough. I devotedly offer my respects to the wonderful Rama-ghatta, where even today all men may see the marks where Balarama began to drag the Yamuna.

attributes: dearer than life to His group of cowherd friends
entered the belly of Aghasura to rescue His friends
slayer of the demon Aghasura
enjoyed transcendental pastimes in the place called Sarpasthali
demon whose belly burned with a great forest fire of most violent poison
friends of Kṛṣṇa for whom He is more dear than life
victims who entered the sinful Aghasura's belly
rescued from the demon by Lord Kṛṣṇa
place where the Lord enjoyed the pastime of killing Aghasura

95.verse_text: Seeing His friends, for whom He is more dear than life, enter the sinful Aghasura’s belly, which burned with a great forest fire of the most violent poison, Lord Krsna also entered the demon, killed it, and rescued His friends. I pray that Sarpasthali, the place where the Lord enjoyed these pastimes, may protect me.

attributes: pleased the mothers of the boys and calves by assuming the forms of their sons
enjoyed the pastime of stealing cowherd boys and calves by Brahma at Vatsaharasthali
creator who stole the cowherd boys and calves
eager to see the glory of the Lord
place where the Lord enjoyed the pastime of stealing cowherd boys and calves

96.verse_text: When, eager to see his master’s glory, the creator Brahma stole the cowherd boys and calves, Lord Krsna pleased the mothers of the boys and calves by assuming the forms of their sons. I worship Vatsaharasthali, where the Lord enjoyed this pastime.

attributes: afraid of having committed a grave offence by stealing cowherd boys and calves
fell down on the ground with tears in his eyes
spoke many wonderful and unprecedented prayers of glorification
frightened seeker of forgiveness through offering prayers to the Lord
gently smiling Lord who is the giver of liberation
wonderful cowherd son of the king of Vraja
king of Vraja and father of the cowherd son Kṛṣṇa
sacred place where frightened Brahmā offered prayers to the Lord

97.verse_text: Afraid that he had committed a grave offence by stealing the cowherd boys and calves, Brahma fell down on the ground, and, with tears in his eyes, spoke many wonderful and unprecedented prayers glorifying gently smiling Lord Krsna, who is both the giver of liberation and the wonderful cowherd son of the king of Vraja. Let us glorify this sacred place, Sesabhirucaturmukha, where frightened Brahma offered prayers to the Lord.

attributes: twelve forests filled with many large lakes, hills, and rivers
adorned with many desire trees and desire creepers
filled with flowers invaded by a great army of intoxicated bumblebees
splendidly beautiful and very dear to Lord Kṛṣṇa
the one to whom the twelve forests of Vṛndāvana are very dear

98.verse_text: Filled with many large lakes, hills, and rivers, with many desire trees and desire creepers, and with many flowers invaded by a great army of bumblebees intoxicated by the sweet aroma, the twelve forests of Vrndavana are splendidly beautiful. I bow down to offer my respects at every moment to these twelve forests, which are very dear to Lord Krsna.

attributes: eternally filled with the nectar of pure devotional love
happily stayed in Vraja for ten months
brought the residents of Vraja back to life by describing Kṛṣṇa's pastimes
considers Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet millions of times more dear than his own life's breath
joyfully proclaimed to the residents of Vraja that Kṛṣṇa had returned
possessor of lotus feet which are the object of Uddhava's deepest love
whose pastimes were described by Uddhava to revive the Vrajavāsīs
place where Uddhava happily resided for ten months
abode of residents who were revived by hearing of Kṛṣṇa's pastimes

99.verse_text: Leaving behind Lord Krsna’s lotus feet, which are millions of times more dear to him than his own life’s breath, Uddhava, eternally filled with the nectar of pure devotional love, happily stayed in Vraja for ten months. Moment by moment he brought the residents of Vraja back to life by describing Lord Krsna’s pastimes. He said to them, “Look! Krsna has returned!” I carry Uddhava’s feet upon my head.

entities.display_name: Devotees of the Lord in Vraja
attributes: supremely pious dear devotees of the Lord residing in Vraja
great demigod entrusted with many weighty duties
sincerely yearns to take birth among the bushes and grass in Vraja
sacred abode where devotees of the Lord reside
holy place where even Lord Brahma yearns to be born as grass

100.verse_text: With great humbleness let me offer my respectful obeisances to the supremely pious dear devotees of the Lord who reside in Vraja. Even though Brahma is a great demigod entrusted with many weighty duties, he sincerely yearns to take birth among the bushes and blades of grass in Vraja.

attributes: possess beautiful lotus feet worshipped with eternally fresh sweetness of pure transcendental love
favourite lake of Queen Rādhārāṇī
sacred shore where great souls reside because of previous worship
queen who considers Rādhā-kunda to be her favourite lake

101.verse_text: Because of having previously worshipped the beautiful lotus feet of Sri Sri Gandharva-Acyuta (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna) with the eternally fresh sweetness of pure transcendental love, some great souls are able to reside on the shore of Queen Radha’s favourite lake (Radha-kunda). These great devotees are my life and soul.

attributes: Lord who holds all creatures in Vraja very dear
Lord who is assisted in His pastimes by creatures of Vraja
residence requested by Brahmā and other exalted personalities
sacred place inhabited by creatures full of transcendental bliss
grass, bushes, insects, and other creatures very dear to Kṛṣṇa
assistants to the Lord in His transcendental pastimes
beings full of eternal transcendental bliss

102.verse_text: The grass, bushes, insects, and other creatures in Vraja are all very dear to Lord Krsna. They assist the Lord in His pastimes. They are full of transcendental bliss. Again and again the scriptures recount the earnest request of Brahma and others to reside in Vraja. For these reasons I bow to offer my respects to all the creatures who reside in Vraja.

attributes: place where the divine couple wanders aimlessly
site of the Lord's transcendental pastimes
divine personality called out to by name during pastimes at Govardhana
divine personality called out to by name during pastimes at Govardhana
possessor of divine pastimes performed upon Govardhana Hill

103.verse_text: When, aimlessly wandering around Govardhana Hill, madly calling out “O Radha! O Krsna!” again and again, and stumbling as I walk, will I sprinkle the places of the Lord’s pastimes with tears from my eyes?

attributes: land whose sweetness cannot be properly or completely understood
transcendental land whose glory is impossible to describe
filled with transcendental love for the land of Vraja
capable of understanding the sweetness of the land of Vraja
filled with transcendental love for the land of Vraja
capable of understanding the sweetness of the land of Vraja

104.verse_text: Neither Brahma, Narada, Siva, or the great devotees with pure love of Krsna are able to properly or completely understand the sweetness of the land of Vraja. Only Lord Balarama and His mother Rohini, who are both filled with transcendental love, are able to understand the land of Vraja. How is it possible to describe the land of Vraja?

attributes: infallible Supreme Personality of Godhead who is the source of pure love
sacred place where the narrator desires to reside eternally
home of residents who might appear ordinary or gossip

105.verse_text: Even if I have the opportunity to bathe in the nectarean ocean of pure love of Krsna, and even if I have the pure devotees of the infallible Supreme Personality of Godhead as my associates, I will not consent to live, even for a single moment, in any sacred place other than Vrajabhumi. Even if the residents of Vraja appear to be ordinary, and even if they fill my ears with useless gossip, I pray that I may remain among them, eternally residing here in Vraja.

attributes: full of love for Lord Kṛṣṇa
expertly worships Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
possessor of feet that are the only goal of life
Lord who is the object of Rūpa-mañjarī's love
expertly worshipped by Rūpa-mañjarī

106.verse_text: The two feet of Srimati Rupa-manjari, who is full of love for Lord Krsna, and who expertly worships Srimati Radharani, are the only goal of my life.

attributes: reside in the land of Vraja surrounded by Their associates
directly seen by those who regularly read these verses with respect
land where the divine couple resides with Their associates
filled with the splendid and charming sweetness of its residents

107.verse_text: These verses, which bear the title Vraja-vilasa-stava, are very graceful and beautiful, filled with the splendid and charming sweetness of the residents of Vraja. They who regularly read these verses with great respect and greedy enthusiasm will be able to directly see the divine couple surrounded by Their associates in this land of Vraja.
